problems.title: Compute the Hypotenuse
problems.prompt:
Calculate a right triangle's hypotenuse.

Background and required method:
Use sqrt(a^2+b^2) for non-negative side lengths.

Implement this interface:
def hypotenuse(a: float, b: float) -> float:

Parameters:
• a (float): First perpendicular side.
• b (float): Second perpendicular side.
problems.signature: def hypotenuse(a: float, b: float) -> float:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
import math

def hypotenuse(a: float, b: float) -> float:
    """Calculate a right triangle's hypotenuse.

    Args:
        a (float): First perpendicular side.
        b (float): Second perpendicular side.

    Returns:
        float: Calculate a right triangle's hypotenuse.
    """
    pass

problems.title: Twelve Days Verse
problems.prompt:
Build one verse of The Twelve Days of Christmas.

Background and required method:
Begin with 'On the {ordinal} day of Christmas my true love sent to me:' and include gifts from day down to first, joining lines with newline characters. Use 'and a Partridge in a Pear Tree.' when day is greater than one.

Implement this interface:
def twelve_days_verse(day: int) -> str:

Parameters:
• day (int): Verse number from 1 through 12.
problems.signature: def twelve_days_verse(day: int) -> str:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
def twelve_days_verse(day: int) -> str:
    """Build one verse of The Twelve Days of Christmas.

    Args:
        day (int): Verse number from 1 through 12.

    Returns:
        str: Build one verse of The Twelve Days of Christmas.
    """
    pass

tests.name: first heading
tests.code: assert twelve_days_verse(1).startswith('On the first day') == True

tests.name: third gifts
tests.code: assert 'Three French Hens' in twelve_days_verse(3) == True

tests.name: cumulative
tests.code: assert 'Two Turtle Doves' in twelve_days_verse(12) == True

problems.title: Center Text
problems.prompt:
Center text within a fixed-width terminal line.

Background and required method:
Add floor((width-len(text))/2) leading spaces. If text is at least width characters, return it unchanged.

• width (int): Total terminal width.

problems.title: Integer String
problems.prompt:
Check whether text represents a base-10 integer.

Background and required method:
Ignore leading and trailing whitespace first. The remaining text is valid when it contains one or more digits, optionally preceded by exactly one + or - sign. Reject a sign without digits, decimal points, internal spaces, and other characters.

Implement this interface:

problems.title: Random Password
problems.prompt:
Generate a random ASCII password.

Background and required method:
Use a local random.Random(seed). Choose a length from 7 through 10 and characters with ASCII codes 33 through 126.

Implement this interface:
def random_password(seed: int | None = None) -> str:

Parameters:
• seed (int | None): Optional deterministic seed.
problems.signature: def random_password(seed: int | None = None) -> str:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
import random

def random_password(seed: int | None = None) -> str:
    """Generate a random ASCII password.

    Args:
        seed (int | None): Optional deterministic seed.

    Returns:
        str: Generate a random ASCII password.
    """
    pass

tests.name: printable
tests.code: assert all(33 <= ord(ch) <= 126 for ch in random_password(2)) == True

problems.title: Random License Plate
problems.prompt:
Generate a random old- or new-style license plate.

Background and required method:
Use a local random.Random(seed). Randomly choose either three uppercase letters plus three digits, or four digits plus three uppercase letters.

Implement this interface:
def random_license_plate(seed: int | None = None) -> str:

Parameters:
• seed (int | None): Optional deterministic seed.
problems.signature: def random_license_plate(seed: int | None = None) -> str:

def random_license_plate(seed: int | None = None) -> str:
    """Generate a random old- or new-style license plate.

    Args:
        seed (int | None): Optional deterministic seed.

    Returns:
        str: Generate a random old- or new-style license plate.
    """
    pass

tests.name: pattern
tests.code: assert (lambda p: (len(p)==6 and p[:3].isalpha() and p[3:].isdigit()) or (len(p)==7 and p[:4].isdigit() and p[4:].isalpha()))(random_license_plate(8)) == True

problems.title: Password Strength
problems.prompt:
Check whether a password meets basic strength requirements.

Background and required method:
A good password contains at least eight characters, at least one uppercase letter, at least one lowercase letter, and at least one digit.

Implement this interface:
def is_good_password(password: str) -> bool:

Parameters:
• password (str): Password to assess.
problems.signature: def is_good_password(password: str) -> bool:

starterFiles.content:

    """Check whether a password meets basic strength requirements.

    Returns:
        bool: Check whether a password meets basic strength requirements.
    """
    pass

problems.title: Random Good Password
problems.prompt:
Generate random passwords until one passes the strength checker.

Background and required method:
Use one local random.Random(seed) stream. Each attempt creates a 7-10 character password from ASCII positions 33-126. A successful password has at least eight characters, one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one digit. Continue until all four conditions hold, then return both the password and the number of attempts.

Implement this interface:
def random_good_password(seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[str, int]:

Parameters:
• seed (int | None): Optional deterministic seed.
problems.signature: def random_good_password(seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[str, int]:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
import random

def random_good_password(seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[str, int]:
    """Generate random passwords until one passes the strength checker.

    Args:
        seed (int | None): Optional deterministic seed.

    Returns:
        tuple[str, int]: Generate random passwords until one passes the strength checker.
    """
    pass

tests.name: strong
tests.code: assert (lambda r: len(r[0])>=8 and any(x.isupper() for x in r[0]) and any(x.islower() for x in r[0]) and any(x.isdigit() for x in r[0]) and r[1]>=1)(random_good_password(1)) == True

tests.name: printable
tests.code: assert all(33 <= ord(ch) <= 126 for ch in random_good_password(7)[0]) == True

problems.title: Hexadecimal and Decimal Digits
problems.prompt:
Convert one digit between hexadecimal and decimal forms.

Background and required method:
Support both conversion directions through one typed interface: a one-character string 0-9 or A-F (case-insensitive) returns its integer value, while an integer 0-15 returns one uppercase hexadecimal digit. Raise ValueError otherwise.

Implement this interface:
def convert_hex_digit(value: str | int) -> int | str:

Parameters:
• value (str | int): Single hexadecimal digit or decimal value 0-15.
problems.signature: def convert_hex_digit(value: str | int) -> int | str:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
def convert_hex_digit(value: str | int) -> int | str:
    """Convert one digit between hexadecimal and decimal forms.

    Args:
        value (str | int): Single hexadecimal digit or decimal value 0-15.

    Returns:
        int | str: Convert one digit between hexadecimal and decimal forms.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If value is outside the supported single-digit range.
    """
    pass

problems.title: Arbitrary Base Conversion
problems.prompt:

Use digits 0-9 and A-F. Convert through a decimal accumulator without int(text, base) or built-in formatting. Return uppercase digits.

• source_base (int): Base used by text, from 2 through 16.
• target_base (int): Desired base, from 2 through 16.

problems.title: Reduce a Fraction
problems.prompt:
Reduce a fraction to lowest terms.

Background and required method:
Divide numerator and denominator by their greatest common divisor. Keep the denominator positive and preserve a zero numerator as 0/1.

Implement this interface:
def reduce_fraction(numerator: int, denominator: int) -> tuple[int, int]:

Parameters:
• numerator (int): Fraction numerator.
• denominator (int): Non-zero fraction denominator.

problems.title: Reduce Recipe Measures
problems.prompt:
Express an imperial recipe volume using the largest practical units.

Background and required method:
Convert amount and unit to teaspoons using 1 tablespoon=3 teaspoons and 1 cup=16 tablespoons. Then convert back to the largest units and return a natural-language string that omits zero-valued units and uses correct singular/plural labels.

Implement this interface:
def reduce_measures(amount: int, unit: str) -> str:

Parameters:
• amount (int): Non-negative count of the supplied unit.
• unit (str): 'cup', 'tablespoon', or 'teaspoon'.
problems.signature: def reduce_measures(amount: int, unit: str) -> str:

starterFiles.content:

problems.title: Magic Dates
problems.prompt:
Determine whether a date is mathematically magic.

Background and required method:
A date is magic when day multiplied by month equals the final two digits of year; June 10, 1960 qualifies because 6*10=60. Implement this predicate first. A complete program can then combine it with a days-in-month function to examine every valid date from 1901 through 2000 and display all matches.

Implement this interface:
def is_magic_date(year: int, month: int, day: int) -> bool:

Parameters:
• year (int): Four-digit year.
• month (int): Month number.
• day (int): Day of month.
problems.signature: def is_magic_date(year: int, month: int, day: int) -> bool:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
def is_magic_date(year: int, month: int, day: int) -> bool:
    """Determine whether a date is mathematically magic.

    Args:
        year (int): Four-digit year.
        month (int): Month number.
        day (int): Day of month.

    Returns:
        bool: Determine whether a date is mathematically magic.
    """
    pass
